title: Strategic Chargeback Window Exploitation with Delivery Confirmation Timing
narrative: Customer completes purchase of high-value electronics ($800-$2,500 ProductCD=W) with verified delivery signature, then files dispute on day 118-120 of 120-day chargeback window. Account shows 8-15 successful transactions over 4-6 months establishing legitimacy, all under $200, then single high-value purchase. Cardholder waits until final 48 hours before deadline to dispute, claiming non-receipt despite delivery confirmation. Pattern includes no previous disputes on account, deliberate timing to limit merchant investigation window, and purchase amount 4-7x higher than historical card1_amt_zscore baseline. Shipping address (addr1) matches billing in all prior transactions, then differs only for disputed item. Communication attempts during dispute window show P_emaildomain remains active but unresponsive to merchant contact.
indicators: Single transaction $800-$2,500 after 8-15 lower-value purchases ($50-$200 range) over 4-6 months
Dispute filed between day 118-120 of 120-day chargeback window
Disputed transaction card1_amt_zscore exceeds 2.5 (4-7x historical average)
addr1 mismatch on disputed transaction only, all prior transactions matched billing
Zero dispute history on card1 across 8-15 previous successful transactions
P_emaildomain shows login activity during dispute period but zero merchant communication responses
ProductCD=W (electronics/high-resale items) on disputed transaction versus ProductCD=C,H on historical purchases
summary: A customer builds trust with 8-15 small purchases over months, then makes one high-value electronics purchase with a different shipping address and files a chargeback in the final 48 hours of the 120-day dispute window despite confirmed delivery.
reasoning: The timing is deliberate—waiting until the last moment limits the merchant's ability to investigate while the earlier pattern of legitimate purchases was designed to avoid suspicion, indicating the customer planned to keep both the item and their money.
indicator_explanations: Cardholder builds trust with small purchases then strikes with one large transaction
Dispute filed at the last possible moment of the chargeback window
Disputed purchase amount far exceeds what this cardholder normally spends
Shipping address suddenly changes only for the high-value disputed transaction
Cardholder never complained about any previous purchases on this card
Email account stays active during dispute but ignores all merchant inquiries
Disputed item is high-resale electronics unlike previous low-value purchase categories
